candidate_problems.text: What specific forms of criticism must a conjecture-and-criticism architecture implement for an LLM-generated explanation to count as surviving criticism better than rivals, rather than merely surviving preference ranking or benchmark selection?
Can an LLM-centered system generate genuinely new explanatory variants in domains where no external empirical test is available, such as mathematics or philosophy, using criticism alone?
What distinguishes a merely associative generation process that later gets filtered by critics from a process that actually explores explanatory structure rather than lexical variation?
Under what conditions would a closed-loop LLM critique system fail by converging on mutually reinforcing but false explanations that appear hard to vary within the loop?
